description: Turd Tracker — A Blazor WASM PWA poo diary with Bristol Stool Scale, tags, calendar view, stats, and PDF export

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to scaffold the Blazor WASM project with dependencies and folder structure so that all subsequent stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Blazor WebAssembly standalone project created targeting .NET 10 with PWA template
MudBlazor NuGet package installed and configured in Program.cs
Blazored.LocalStorage NuGet package installed and configured in Program.cs
Folder structure created: Pages/, Components/, Models/, Services/
Project builds and runs successfully with dotnet run
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build requires DOTNET_CLI_USE_MSBUILD_SERVER=0 on macOS ARM64

userStories.title: Data model and local storage service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a data model for diary entries and a service to persist them in local storage so that other features can store and retrieve data.
acceptanceCriteria: DiaryEntry model class created with properties: Id (Guid), Timestamp (DateTime), BristolType (int 1-7), Notes (string), Tags (List<string>)
IDiaryService interface created with methods: GetAllAsync, GetByIdAsync, GetByDateAsync, AddAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync
DiaryService implementation uses Blazored.LocalStorage to persist entries
Service registered in DI container in Program.cs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bristol Stool Scale selector component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the Bristol Stool Scale types with images and descriptions so that I can accurately classify my stool.
acceptanceCriteria: BristolScaleSelector component created in Components/
All 7 Bristol Stool Scale types displayed with type number, name, and short description
Standard medical chart images included in wwwroot/images/ (or placeholder SVGs if not available)
Each type shows its image alongside the text
Selected type is visually highlighted with a distinct border or background
Only one type can be selected at a time
Component exposes a SelectedType parameter with EventCallback for two-way binding
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: wasm-tools workload must be installed for build to succeed on macOS ARM64

userStories.title: Log entry form page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly log a bowel movement with auto-populated date/time and Bristol type selection so that I can track my habits.
acceptanceCriteria: New page at /log route
Date and time fields auto-populated to current date/time
Date and time can be manually adjusted
Bristol Stool Scale selector component integrated into the form
Bristol type selection is required before saving
Optional free-text notes field available
Save button calls DiaryService.AddAsync and navigates to diary list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Tags input component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add optional tags to my entries so I can spot correlations later.
acceptanceCriteria: TagInput component created in Components/
User can type a custom tag and press Enter to add it
Tags display as removable chips below the input
Common/recent tags shown as quick-select chips (pulled from existing entries via DiaryService)
Multiple tags can be added per entry
Component integrated into the log entry form page
Tags are optional — form can be saved without them
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diary list view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a chronological list of all my entries so I can review my history.
acceptanceCriteria: Diary list page at / (index) route
Entries displayed in reverse chronological order (newest first)
Each entry shows: date/time, Bristol type number with icon/indicator, tags as chips, and truncated notes
Tapping an entry navigates to a detail view page (/entry/{id})
Empty state message shown when no entries exist
FAB or prominent button to navigate to /log
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Entry detail view with edit and delete
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view, edit, and delete a diary entry so I can correct mistakes or remove test data.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail page at /entry/{id} route showing all entry fields
Edit button toggles fields to editable mode (date/time, Bristol type, notes, tags)
Save button persists changes via DiaryService.UpdateAsync
Delete button shows MudBlazor confirmation dialog before deleting
After delete, navigates back to diary list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Main navigation layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent navigation so I can switch between diary, calendar, stats, and settings.
acceptanceCriteria: MudBlazor NavMenu or bottom navigation bar with items: Diary, Calendar, Stats, Settings
Navigation highlights the current active page
Layout is responsive — bottom nav on mobile, side nav on desktop
All existing pages accessible via navigation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Calendar view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my entries on a monthly calendar so I can visualise frequency at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar page at /calendar route
Monthly calendar grid displayed with previous/next month navigation
Days with entries show a count badge or indicator dot(s)
Clicking a day shows a list of entries for that day below the calendar
Current day is visually highlighted
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark mode default with light mode toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to default to dark mode and let me switch to light mode.
acceptanceCriteria: App loads in dark mode by default on first visit using MudBlazor dark theme
Theme toggle switch available in settings page or app header
Theme preference persists in local storage across sessions
Both dark and light themes render correctly across all pages
Dark theme uses muted dark greys, not pure black
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Stats — frequency over time chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how often I log entries over time so I can track regularity.
acceptanceCriteria: Stats page at /stats route
Bar or line chart showing entries per day or per week
Selectable time range (last 7 days, 30 days, 90 days)
Chart renders correctly with zero, few, and many entries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Stats — Bristol type distribution chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the breakdown of my Bristol types so I can understand my typical patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Pie or bar chart on /stats page showing count per Bristol type (1-7)
Chart uses consistent colours matching Bristol type indicators elsewhere in the app
Chart renders correctly with zero, few, and many entries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Stats — time of day patterns chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what times of day I most commonly log entries so I can spot habits.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart on /stats page showing entry distribution by hour of day
Uses a bar chart or histogram with 24-hour buckets
Chart renders correctly with zero, few, and many entries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Export diary to PDF
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export my diary data to PDF so I can share it with my doctor.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button accessible from diary list or settings page
Optional date range filter for the export
Generates a printable PDF or print-friendly HTML page with all entry details (date/time, Bristol type, notes, tags)
PDF layout is clean and professional
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PWA icons, manifest, and offline support
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install the app on my phone and use it offline so it feels like a native app.
acceptanceCriteria: App icon set created and referenced in manifest.json (192px and 512px)
manifest.json has correct name, short_name, theme_color, and background_color
Service worker caches app shell for offline use
App works fully offline after first load
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build requires DOTNET_HOST_PATH=$(which dotnet) in addition to DOTNET_CLI_USE_MSBUILD_SERVER=0 on macOS ARM64
